did.unittitle: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library records

origination.label: creator

did.physdesc: Subject files, accession records, and photographs.
did.abstract: Subject files, accession records, and photographs created by the Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library at the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The library at the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine was founded on September 21, 1897. It was originally named after New York State governor, Roswell P. Flower, but became the Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library in 1992 when it was renamed to recognize the generosity of Dr. and Mrs. Isidor I. Sprecher.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Subject files, accession records, and photographs created by the Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library at the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIAL
relatedmaterial.p: See also the John B. Taylor and Simon Henry Gage correspondence, #24-4-m.1021, for more information about early funding for the library

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Subject Files Box: 1 and 2
Series III. Accession Records, Requisitions Boxes: 1-3

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library Records, # 24-15-3366. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
